photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Fair condition. Brick appears original and in reasonable shape. Two separate entrances visible indicating semi-detached/duplex. Number '112' visible on right unit. Chain-link fence at front. Bins and utility equipment in front yard.
photo_observations.window_details: Replacement windows, likely vinyl or aluminum. Simple rectangular openings without decorative brick surrounds.
photo_observations.door_details: Two separate entrance doors for duplex units. Dark coloured modern replacement doors. Small concrete stoops with steps.
photo_observations.porch_details: Small concrete stoops at each entrance with 3-4 steps
photo_observations.decorative_details: Minimal - plain brick duplex with white painted gable/upper trim
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Simple gable, white painted upper gable area
photo_observations.storefront_description: No storefront is present; this is a straightforward duplex residence with two separate domestic entrances at grade.
photo_observations.signage_observed: '112' house number visible. Speed limit '30' sign and one-way arrow sign on utility pole in front.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Replacement vinyl windows throughout. Likely some modern repairs to brickwork. Chain-link and partial wood fence.
photo_observations.notes: Semi-detached brick duplex, plain vernacular style. Two units with separate entrances. Utility pole partially obstructs view.

facade_description: 112 Oxford St area (brick duplex) presents a house-form facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a simple gable, white painted upper gable area, consistent with its house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 3, 4 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double hung, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
